Member-only story

Six snippets that look safe. The race is always in the gap between two steps.

8 min read
Just now

--

I ask one version of this question in almost every interview now, and it’s not because I want to trap people. It’s because “thread-safe” is the word everyone uses and almost nobody can defend. Candidates say “I’d wrap it in a queue” the way people say “I’d add an index” to a database question — a memorized reflex that falls apart the second you ask why, or change the snippet slightly.
So here’s the same thing, as a self-test. Six snippets, each looks more harmless than the last. For each one, commit to an answer before you read on — safe, or not safe — and more importantly, why. The “why” is the entire point. Plenty of people guess “not safe” correctly and still can’t explain what actually goes wrong, and in an interview that’s the same as not knowing.
Friend link:
Snippet 1: the one everyone gets right for the wrong reason
class Counter {
var value = 0
func increment() { value += 1 }
}let counter = Counter()
DispatchQueue.concurrentPerform(iterations: 1000) { _ in
counter.increment()
}
// is…
